key_insight: Transition-metal substitution turns Ga2O2 from a weak gas adsorber into a tunable platform for selective room-temperature sensing or permanent toxic-gas scavenging.
engineering_relevance: These doped monolayers could enable low-power atmospheric sensors and reusable detoxification materials for industrial and environmental monitoring.
method: First-principles DFT screening of seven TM substitutions: Pd, Zn, Zr, Mo, Ag, Ti, and Pt
Stability checks using formation/binding energies, lattice distortion analysis, and molecular dynamics simulations
Adsorption and selectivity evaluation against eight toxic industrial gases and three environmental gases
evidence_signal: The abstract reports negative formation and binding energies, minimal structural distortion, and up to eight-order-of-magnitude conductivity enhancement for Ag-substituted Ga2O2 when exposed to NO.
watch_item: Selectivity and capture performance are computational predictions, so experimental synthesis, defect control, and ambient-condition validation will be critical before deployment.
paper_title: Transition-Metal Tailored $Ga_{2}O_{2}$ Monolayer: From Room-Temperature Gas Sensing to Chemical Scavenging
